title: nditer.multi_index setter segfaults when a Python __getitem__ raises
category: re-entrancy NULL-deref segfault across the Python ↔ C boundary (unchecked PySequence_GetItem)
crash_site: interpreter segfault inside NumPy's C nditer code while assigning to nditer.multi_index using a Python object whose element access raises an exception.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the user's Python __getitem__ (it merely raises — not the bug) and the iterator-advance path. The defect is npyiter_multi_index_set not checking PySequence_GetItem for NULL when the call raised.
ground_truth.fix_summary: npyiter_multi_index_set called PySequence_GetItem on the supplied index object and used the result without a NULL check; if the object's __getitem__ raises, PySequence_GetItem returns NULL and the C code dereferenced it -> segfault. Fix: add a NULL check after PySequence_GetItem and propagate the error.

admission.note: Authored from PR #31314 body+diff. crash != cause: the segfault is in the C nditer setter; the cause is a Python callback (__getitem__) raising, returning NULL from PySequence_GetItem, which npyiter_multi_index_set used unchecked. Decoy = the Python __getitem__ itself. Single file -> watch contamination. Python re-entrancy <-> C error handling. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/31314 — Fix segfault in nditer.multi_index when __getitem__ raises; fix fa6d67432de1, base a9c745972cd7.
